name: Lucid x Bay Area Private College Counselors
brief: Find founder/owner-level college counselors running Bay Area private practices that serve STEM-heavy 'try-hard' high schoolers targeting Ivy/MIT/Stanford. We want to refer those students to Lucid Academy's AI/ML research program. Skip big-box franchises and junior analysts — we want the person whose name is on the door, who personally advises families and decides which outside programs to recommend.

offer.summary: Project-based AI/ML coaching for high schoolers — 1:1 mentorship from ISEF/Regeneron alumni, original research projects, and competition + internship placement.
proof: 125+ students shipped from basic programming to original AI projects and national competition awards
Mentors are ISEF, Regeneron STS, and top-lab researchers who guide students 1:1 through publishable work
Students place at university research labs and win at ISEF, Regeneron, JSHS, and AI fairs
offer.icp_for_referral: Driven STEM-track high schoolers (sophomores/juniors) at competitive Bay Area schools who already code and want a real research project, competition result, or lab internship for Ivy/MIT/Stanford applications.

search.query: Independent boutique college counseling and admissions consulting firms in the San Francisco Bay Area founded and led by their owner, serving STEM-focused students applying to Ivy League, MIT, Stanford, and Caltech.
criteria: Independent / boutique college counseling practice (not Crimson, IvyWise, C2, Princeton Review, or other large franchises)
Headquartered or primarily operating in the San Francisco Bay Area (SF, Peninsula, South Bay, East Bay)
Founder, owner, or principal counselor is the public face of the firm and personally takes clients
Explicitly serves high-achieving STEM students or markets Ivy / MIT / Stanford / Caltech / CMU outcomes
Recommends or curates outside enrichment, research, or summer programs for students
Has a working website and at least one direct contact channel (email or contact form)

enrichments.label: founder_name
enrichments.description: Full name of the firm's founder, owner, or principal counselor.

enrichments.label: founder_email
enrichments.description: Direct work email of the founder; fall back to firm's primary contact email.

enrichments.label: stem_focus_evidence
enrichments.description: Quote or sentence from the site showing STEM / Ivy / MIT / Stanford specialization.

enrichments.label: outcomes_or_admits
enrichments.description: Specific admissions outcomes or schools listed (e.g. 'MIT 2024, Stanford 2023').

enrichments.label: service_area_city
enrichments.description: Primary Bay Area city or region the firm serves (e.g. Palo Alto, Cupertino, SF).

email_angles.angle: Plug a hole in the STEM applicant package
email_angles.hook: The hardest part of the Bay Area STEM applicant isn't the GPA — it's giving them something real to point at.
email_angles.body_outline:
- Note their STEM/Ivy positioning and that their students usually have the grades + scores already
- Lucid pairs those students 1:1 with ISEF/Regeneron-caliber mentors to ship an original AI project and target a real competition or lab placement
- Offer to send a 1-page referral sheet they can hand to families, plus a partner code so we can track who comes from them
email_angles.cta: Worth a 15-min intro call this week or next so you can vet us before recommending?

email_angles.angle: Built for the kid who already codes
email_angles.hook: Most enrichment programs are too soft for the Gunn/Harker/Mission San Jose kid who's been coding since 8th grade.
email_angles.body_outline:
- Acknowledge they likely turn down a lot of summer-program pitches because their students have outgrown intro classes
- Lucid is the opposite problem: structured 1:1 research with mentors who've actually won ISEF/Regeneron, output is a real project + paper, not a certificate
- 125+ students through, concrete examples of competition wins and lab internships we can share
email_angles.cta: Open to a quick call so I can walk you through two recent student outcomes and you can decide if it's a fit?

email_angles.hook: Reaching out to you directly because you're the one who actually decides which outside programs your families hear about.
email_angles.body_outline:
- Skip the BD pitch — explain Lucid in two sentences and what kind of student it's right for
- Be explicit: we don't want to be on a generic resource list, we want a real referral relationship with counselors whose judgment we trust
- Offer a no-cost diagnostic call for one of their current juniors so they can see the program quality firsthand
email_angles.cta: Can I send over our mentor roster and one sample student deliverable so you can take a look on your own time?

qa_checklist: Counselor replies asking for the referral sheet, mentor roster, or sample deliverable
Counselor names a specific current student who might be a fit
Founder responds personally (not assistant/analyst), confirming we hit the right level
Zero replies from large franchises like Crimson/IvyWise/C2 — means our filtering worked
